questions.question: Under 10 CFR 34.49, the survey of the exposure device and guide tube performed after each exposure must be made:
options: after the equipment has been broken down and loaded, to confirm nothing was left behind
while approaching the device, to confirm the source returned to its shielded position before film is exchanged or the head is repositioned
only once per workday, immediately before the device is placed in storage
from inside the truck cab using the area monitor, to keep the radiographer at maximum distance
questions.explanation: 10 CFR 34.49(b) requires the post-exposure survey of the device and guide tube to be made while approaching them, determining the sealed source is shielded BEFORE anyone exchanges film, repositions the exposure head, or dismantles equipment. Surveying after teardown defeats the purpose — it must catch a stuck source while distance still protects you.

questions.question: A radiographer records 'OK' in the boundary column of a survey report instead of the measured value. Why does this fail as a compliance record?
options: Inspectors require all entries to be made in ink rather than abbreviations
A checkmark cannot be reconstructed or defended later; a numeric value with units and location is the actual evidence that boundaries and public-dose limits were met
'OK' is acceptable only if the survey meter calibration is current
The licensee, not the radiographer, is required to fill in numeric readings
questions.explanation: The heart of the report is numbers with units and locations — '1.6 mR/h at the north boundary rope, 14:05' proves compliance, while 'OK' proves nothing and cannot be reconstructed in an inspection two years later. The discipline is to record what the meter read, never a checkmark or what you expected.

questions.question: A boundary survey report was later used, together with dosimetry data, to assess an individual's dose after a suspected overexposure. Under 10 CFR 20.2103, how long must that survey record be retained?
options: 3 years after the record is made, like a routine area survey
5 years after the investigation is formally closed
Until the Commission terminates the license
90 days, after which the dosimetry record alone suffices
questions.explanation: Routine survey records are kept 3 years under 20.2103(a), but 20.2103(b) requires any survey used — alone or with individual monitoring data — to assess an individual's dose equivalent to be retained until the NRC terminates the license. An ordinary boundary report becomes a permanent record the moment it feeds an overexposure evaluation.

questions.question: Which single source contributes the most to the average American's natural background dose of about 3.1 mSv per year?
options: Cosmic radiation, which roughly doubles at Denver's altitude
Terrestrial gamma rays from soil, brick, and concrete
Potassium-40 and carbon-14 built into body tissue
Radon and thoron decay products inhaled into the lungs
questions.explanation: Per NCRP Report No. 160, inhaled radon and thoron decay products average about 2.28 mSv per year — by far the largest natural contributor. Cosmic (~0.33), internal emitters (~0.29), and terrestrial gamma (~0.21 mSv) are each much smaller, though all four are excluded from the occupational dose record.

questions.question: The annual occupational TEDE limit of 5 rem, converted to SI units, equals:
options: 5 mSv
50 mSv
500 mSv
0.5 mSv
questions.explanation: Because 1 Sv = 100 rem, 5 rem = 0.05 Sv = 50 mSv. Sliding this decimal is one of the most common and most serious unit errors a radiographer can make; the public limit of 0.1 rem likewise equals 1 mSv (100 mrem).

questions.question: A leak-test wipe on an Ir-192 exposure device shows 0.008 µCi of removable activity. Under 10 CFR 34.27, the required action is:
options: None — log the value and re-wipe at the next six-month interval, since daily survey readings are normal
Treat the source as leaking: immediately withdraw the device from use and report it, because the result meets or exceeds 0.005 µCi (185 Bq)
Continue use but shorten the leak-test interval to three months
Decontaminate the wipe area and resume, since 0.008 µCi is below the threshold
questions.explanation: 10 CFR 34.27 sets the leaking-source threshold at 0.005 µCi (185 Bq) of removable activity, and 0.008 µCi exceeds it. The device comes out of service immediately for decontamination and repair or disposal — a dose-rate survey cannot clear it, because a survey meter measures dose rate, not removable material.

questions.question: A radiographer finishes a shift with a sealed, leak-tested Ir-192 source; his badge reads 25 mrem. Which statement is correct?
options: He is mildly radioactive and should keep distance from family until the activity decays
His coveralls are contaminated and must be surveyed and bagged before he leaves
He received a radiation dose but carries no radioactive material — he is neither contaminated nor radioactive
Showering promptly will remove most of the 25 mrem he received
questions.explanation: Gamma exposure deposits energy; it transfers no material, and at radiography energies it cannot induce radioactivity (that takes neutrons). Dose is not a substance — there is nothing to wash off and no hazard to those around him. Radiation is energy in flight; contamination is radioactive material in the wrong place.

questions.question: For X and gamma radiation, most biological damage to a cell occurs by which mechanism?
options: Direct action — the photon strikes and breaks the DNA molecule itself
Indirect action — the photon splits a water molecule into free radicals that then attack the DNA
Neutron activation of phosphorus atoms in the DNA backbone
Heat deposited as the photon slows and stops in tissue
questions.explanation: Cells are roughly 70-80% water, so a photon is far more likely to ionize water, producing reactive free radicals (notably ·OH) that diffuse to and damage the DNA. Direct hits on DNA do occur but account for the minority of X/gamma damage; direct action dominates only for densely ionizing alpha and neutrons.

questions.question: Which statement correctly distinguishes a deterministic (somatic) effect from a stochastic effect?
options: Deterministic effects have no threshold; stochastic effects appear only above a threshold dose

Deterministic effects come from cell killing, have a dose threshold, and grow in severity with dose; stochastic effects come from a misrepaired cell, are assumed to have no threshold, and grow in probability with dose
Both rise in severity with dose, but only deterministic effects are delayed by years
Stochastic effects such as skin burns are prompt; deterministic effects such as cancer are delayed
questions.explanation: Deterministic (somatic) effects — burns, blood-count drops, cataracts, ARS — result from killing many cells, have a threshold, and worsen in severity with dose. Stochastic effects — cancer and heritable defects — arise from a single surviving misrepaired cell, are assumed to have no safe threshold, and increase in probability (not severity) with dose. Dose limits prevent the first; ALARA minimizes the second.

questions.question: According to the law of Bergonié and Tribondeau, which group of tissues is the MOST radiosensitive — and the reason the whole-body limit (5 rem) is ten times stricter than the skin/extremity limit (50 rem)?
options: Muscle, adult bone, and mature nerve cells, because they are large and water-rich
Skin, lung, and kidney, because they receive the dose first
Bone marrow and lymphocytes, gonads, intestinal lining, and the lens of the eye, because they are rapidly dividing, undifferentiated, and have a long dividing future
Cartilage and tendon, because they regenerate slowly after injury
questions.explanation: Bergonié–Tribondeau ties radiosensitivity to a high mitotic rate, lack of differentiation, and a long dividing future. Bone marrow and lymphocytes, gonadal cells, the intestinal lining, and the eye lens lead the ranking — which is why a falling blood count is the earliest sign of serious exposure, and why the 5 rem whole-body limit protecting that tissue is far stricter than the 50 rem skin limit.
